title: Minimum induced drag of superelliptical closed lifting systems with a loaded central body — paper, solver, and data
description: Trefftz-plane minimum-induced-drag study of closed (ring) lifting systems, validated against Munk (planar, e=1), Prandtl (box wing, e~1.46 at h/b=0.2), and the classical circular ring (e=2). Results: the elliptical ring follows e = 1 + h/b (Richardson-extrapolated residual 1e-6); the superellipse family between ellipse and box shows a p=4 'squircle' captures ~73-76% of the box benefit; a central lifting body is cheapest when it carries under ~15% of the lift, hugs an arc of the ring, and is wide. A finite-chord vortex-lattice cross-check (sharing the same Trefftz functionals) shows untwisted rings achieve 98.6-100% of the ideal efficiency, with sub-degree twist closing the gap. Includes full Python source (Trefftz optimiser + VLM), all run scripts, data tables, and the compiled manuscript. AI-assistance disclosure: the numerical implementation and manuscript draft were produced with substantial assistance from an AI system (Anthropic Claude) under the author's direction.
